title: Computer System Architecture
description: Practice year-wise MCQs from 2018 to 2026 with explanations and answer review.

questions.prompt: Which component of the CPU performs arithmetic and logical operations?
questions.explanation: The arithmetic logic unit is responsible for arithmetic and logical computations.
A basic instruction cycle involves fetching an instruction and executing it.

questions.prompt: Which memory is fastest among the following?
questions.explanation: Registers are built into the CPU and are the fastest storage locations.

options: Executing one instruction at a time
questions.explanation: Pipelining allows multiple instruction stages to proceed concurrently.

questions.prompt: Which addressing mode uses the address stored in a register?
questions.explanation: Register indirect addressing uses a register that contains the effective address.
Cache sits between the CPU and main memory to reduce access time.

questions.prompt: An interrupt is a signal that:
questions.explanation: Interrupts notify the processor that an event needs immediate handling.

questions.prompt: The primary purpose of a control unit is to:
questions.explanation: The control unit directs the operation of the processor and other components.
Microprogrammed control stores control logic as microinstructions in control memory.
